answer:
The carrier amplitude remains 4 volts, and the carrier frequency is \(2 \text{ MHz} = 2\pi \times 10^6\) rad/s. The new modulating signal has amplitude 8 V and frequency 2 kHz, so the frequency deviation increases proportionally to the amplitude change, giving a deviation of \(1 \text{ kHz} \times (8/2) = 4 \text{ kHz}\), and the modulation index becomes \(4 \text{ kHz} / 2 \text{ kHz} = 2\). Thus, the expression is \(v(t) = 4\cos(4\pi \times 10^6 t + 2 \sin(2\pi \times 2000 t))\), which matches option A.  

\boxed{A}
